attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: place microwaved mug on shelves to the left of counter
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a kitchen environment with multiple objects and surfaces relevant to the planning task. The primary goal is to place a microwaved mug on shelves to the left of the counter.

1. Objects and Their Types:

- Microwave: A silver microwave oven mounted above the stove. It is a receptacle and is openable. Its control panel with numeric buttons is visible on the right side. The interior is partially visible through the glass door, showing a white mug inside. The microwave is currently closed and not toggled on (not isOn).

- Mug: A white ceramic mug located inside the microwave. It is an object (not a receptacle). It is currently not heated (not isHot) and not clean (not isClean). It is not being held by the agent.

- Stove: A gas stove with black grates and a brown pan resting on the right burner. The stove is not a receptacle and is not relevant to the current task.

- Counter: A beige marble countertop running beneath the microwave and extending to the right. It supports various appliances and is not a receptacle.

- Coffee Machine: A red and black coffee machine on the counter to the right. It is an object and not a receptacle. It is not toggled on.

- Toaster: A red toaster located to the left of the coffee machine on the counter. It is an object and not a receptacle.

- Shelves: A black, two-tiered shelf unit located to the left of the counter, against the wall. It is a receptacle and can hold objects. The top shelf holds a red apple, a brown egg, and a silver spoon. The bottom shelf is empty. The shelves are not openable.

- Table: A white table with metal legs to the right of the shelves. It holds a brown loaf of bread. It is not a receptacle.

- Sink: Not visible in any of the images; therefore, no sink-related predicates can be asserted.

- Fridge: Not visible in any of the images; therefore, no fridge-related predicates can be asserted.

- Knife: Not visible in any of the images; therefore, no knife-related predicates can be asserted.

2. Quantities:

- Receptacles: 3 (microwave, shelves, and implicitly the sink/fridge if they existed — but only microwave and shelves are visible and usable).
- Objects: At least 7 (mug, pan, coffee machine, toaster, apple, egg, spoon, loaf of bread — some are on shelves or table).
- Agents: 1 (the robot, initially not at any location).

3. Spatial Relationships:

- The microwave is mounted above the stove and is directly above the counter.
- The mug is inside the microwave (inReceptacle mug microwave).
- The shelves are to the left of the counter and adjacent to the wall.
- The apple, egg, and spoon are on the top shelf (inReceptacle apple shelves, inReceptacle egg shelves, inReceptacle spoon shelves).
- The loaf of bread is on the table, which is to the right of the shelves.
- The coffee machine and toaster are on the counter to the right of the stove.
- The pan is on the stove’s right burner.

4. Current State of Relevant Objects:

- Microwave: closed (not opened), not toggled on (not isOn).
- Mug: inside microwave (inReceptacle mug microwave), not heated (not isHot), not clean (not isClean), not held (not holds agent mug).
- Shelves: not openable (openable shelves is false), top shelf occupied, bottom shelf empty.
- Agent: not at any location (not atLocation agent ?any).

5. Visual Properties:

- Microwave: silver with black control panel, glass door with reflections.
- Mug: white, cylindrical, visible through microwave door.
- Shelves: black metal frame, two tiers, top shelf holds colorful items (red apple, brown egg, silver spoon).
- Counter: beige marble with dark veining.
- Coffee machine: red and black with a circular power button.
- Toaster: red with slots on top.
- Pan: brown, square, on stove.
- Table: white surface, thin metal legs.
- Apple: bright red, round.
- Egg: brown, oval.
- Spoon: silver, metallic.
- Loaf of bread: brown, rectangular.

The robot must first navigate to the microwave, open it, retrieve the mug, heat it, and then place it on the empty bottom shelf of the black shelf unit to the left of the counter. The shelves are accessible and can hold the mug. The microwave must be opened before the mug can be retrieved.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem microwave_mug_shelf)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent
        microwave - receptacle
        shelves - receptacle
        mug - object
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        loaf - object
        pan - object
        coffee_machine - object
        toaster - object
        stove - object
        counter - object
        table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent microwave))
        (not (atLocation agent shelves))
        (not (atLocation agent mug))
        (not (atLocation agent apple))
        (not (atLocation agent egg))
        (not (atLocation agent spoon))
        (not (atLocation agent loaf))
        (not (atLocation agent pan))
        (not (atLocation agent coffee_machine))
        (not (atLocation agent toaster))
        (not (atLocation agent stove))
        (not (atLocation agent counter))
        (not (atLocation agent table))

        ; Microwave is openable and currently closed
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))

        ; Shelves are not openable
        (not (openable shelves))

        ; Mug is inside microwave
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)

        ; Mug is not heated, not clean, not held
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (holds agent mug))
        (not (holdsAny agent))

        ; Apple, egg, spoon are on shelves (top shelf)
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)

        ; Loaf is on table
        (inReceptacle loaf table)

        ; Pan is on stove
        (inReceptacle pan stove)

        ; Microwave is not toggled on
        (not (isOn microwave))

        ; Other objects are not relevant for heating/cleaning/slicing
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; Mug must be heated
            (isHot mug)
            ; Mug must be placed on shelves (bottom shelf, which is empty)
            (inReceptacle mug shelves)
            ; Mug is no longer in microwave
            (not (inReceptacle mug microwave))
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem microwave_mug_shelf)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent
        microwave - receptacle
        shelves - receptacle
        mug - object
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        loaf - object
        pan - object
        coffee_machine - object
        toaster - object
        stove - object
        counter - object
        table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent microwave))
        (not (atLocation agent shelves))
        (not (atLocation agent mug))
        (not (atLocation agent apple))
        (not (atLocation agent egg))
        (not (atLocation agent spoon))
        (not (atLocation agent loaf))
        (not (atLocation agent pan))
        (not (atLocation agent coffee_machine))
        (not (atLocation agent toaster))
        (not (atLocation agent stove))
        (not (atLocation agent counter))
        (not (atLocation agent table))

        ; Microwave is openable and currently closed
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))

        ; Shelves are not openable
        (not (openable shelves))

        ; Mug is inside microwave
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)

        ; Mug is not heated, not clean, not held
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (holds agent mug))
        (not (holdsAny agent))

        ; Apple, egg, spoon are on shelves (top shelf)
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)

        ; Loaf is on table
        (inReceptacle loaf table)

        ; Pan is on stove
        (inReceptacle pan stove)

        ; Microwave is not toggled on
        (not (isOn microwave))

        ; Other objects are not relevant for heating/cleaning/slicing
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; Mug must be heated
            (isHot mug)
            ; Mug must be placed on shelves (bottom shelf, which is empty)
            (inReceptacle mug shelves)
            ; Mug is no longer in microwave
            (not (inReceptacle mug microwave))
        )
    )
)

Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Microwave: A silver microwave oven mounted above a stovetop. It is a receptacle and is openable. Its control panel is visible on the right side with numeric buttons. The interior is partially visible through the glass door, showing a white mug inside.
   - Mug: A white ceramic mug located inside the microwave. It is an object (not a receptacle). It is currently inside the microwave, so the predicate (inReceptacle mug microwave) holds.
   - Stovetop: A black gas stovetop with grates, located directly beneath the microwave. It is not a receptacle.
   - Pan: A brown frying pan resting on the stovetop. It is an object.
   - Counter: A brown marbled countertop extending beneath the microwave and stovetop. It is not a receptacle.
   - Shelves: A black two-tiered shelf unit located to the left of the counter (visible in the third image). It is a receptacle and can hold objects. It is positioned next to a white table.
   - Apple: A red apple sitting on the top shelf of the black shelf unit. It is an object.
   - Egg: A brown egg on the top shelf next to the apple. It is an object.
   - Spoon: A metallic spoon on the top shelf next to the egg. It is an object.
   - Bread loaf: A brown loaf of bread on the white table to the right of the shelf unit. It is an object.
   - Coffee machine: A red and black coffee machine on a separate counter (second image). It is not a receptacle and not relevant to the current task.
   - Toaster: A red toaster next to the coffee machine. It is not a receptacle and not relevant.
   - Trash bin: A green hexagonal trash bin under the counter with the coffee machine. It is not a receptacle in the domain and not relevant.

2. Quantities:
   - Microwave: 1
   - Mug: 1
   - Shelves (receptacle): 1 (the black two-tiered unit)
   - Apple: 1
   - Egg: 1
   - Spoon: 1
   - Bread loaf: 1
   - Pan: 1

3. Spatial relationships (matching domain predicates):
   - (inReceptacle mug microwave) — The mug is inside the microwave.
   - The microwave is mounted above the stovetop.
   - The black shelf unit is to the left of the counter where the stovetop is located.
   - The apple, egg, and spoon are on the top shelf of the black shelf unit.
   - The bread loaf is on a white table to the right of the black shelf unit.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - Microwave: Currently closed (not opened). It is not specified as being on or toggled, but the mug inside may be intended to be heated.
   - Mug: Inside the microwave, not held by any agent, not cleaned, not sliced, not cooled, and not yet heated (assuming the task is to heat it).
   - Shelves: Empty on the lower tier; the upper tier holds the apple, egg, and spoon. It is a receptacle and can receive the mug after heating.
   - The robot is initially not at any object’s location, so it must first navigate to the microwave to interact with the mug.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - Microwave: Silver with a dark glass door and a black control panel with white numbers.
   - Mug: White, cylindrical, visible through the microwave door.
   - Stovetop: Black metal grates on a white surface.
   - Pan: Brown, square-shaped, with a black handle, sitting on the stovetop.
   - Counter: Brown with a marbled texture.
   - Shelves: Black metal frame with two tiers; the top tier holds a red apple, a brown egg, and a silver spoon.
   - Apple: Bright red.
   - Egg: Light brown.
   - Spoon: Metallic silver.
   - Bread loaf: Brown crust, rectangular.
   - Coffee machine: Red body with black accents and a silver power button.
   - Toaster: Red with black slots.
   - Trash bin: Green, hexagonal, under the counter.

Note: The task requires the robot to heat the mug in the microwave and then place it on the shelves to the left of the counter. The shelves are the black two-tiered unit visible in the third image, which is adjacent to the counter area with the stovetop and microwave.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem microwave_mug_to_shelves)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        mug - object
        shelves - receptacle
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        bread - object
        pan - object
        stovetop - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)
        (atLocation robot microwave) ; Assuming robot starts near microwave for task initiation
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)
        ; The shelves are a receptacle and can hold the mug
        ; The pan is on the stovetop, but not relevant to the goal
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug shelves)
        (isHot mug)
    ))
)
```

    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        mug - object
        shelves - receptacle
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        bread - object
        pan - object
        stovetop - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)
        (atLocation robot microwave) ; Assuming robot starts near microwave for task initiation
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)
        ; The shelves are a receptacle and can hold the mug
        ; The pan is on the stovetop, but not relevant to the goal

    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug shelves)
        (isHot mug)

)

        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )

    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

```pddl
(define (problem microwave_mug_to_shelves)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        mug - object
        shelves - receptacle
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        bread - object
        pan - object
        stovetop - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)
        (atLocation robot microwave) ; Assuming robot starts near microwave for task initiation
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)
        ; The shelves are a receptacle and can hold the mug
        ; The pan is on the stovetop, but not relevant to the goal
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug shelves)
        (isHot mug)
    ))
)
```

(define (problem microwave_mug_to_shelves)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        mug - object
        shelves - receptacle
        apple - object
        egg - object
        spoon - object
        bread - object
        pan - object
        stovetop - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (inReceptacle mug microwave)
        (atLocation robot microwave) ; Assuming robot starts near microwave for task initiation
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (inReceptacle apple shelves)
        (inReceptacle egg shelves)
        (inReceptacle spoon shelves)
        ; The shelves are a receptacle and can hold the mug
        ; The pan is on the stovetop, but not relevant to the goal
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug shelves)
        (isHot mug)
    ))
)
